workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you work with me on escalation triage for Litware Inc?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 09:46 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Litware Inc

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 14:17 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 08:55 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-C trap (Manager brief reuses customer draft language); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-C failure mode (Manager brief reuses customer draft language)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-C -- Manager brief reuses customer draft language
